title: Advanced Algorithm Engineering Capstone · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Build and defend a planning engine across graph, optimization, and uncertain-load requirements. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an advanced algorithm system with proof and operations packet with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare exact, approximate, randomized, and baseline candidates under one evidence model.
Connect proof obligations to executable invariants, oracle comparisons, and counterexamples.
Enforce time, memory, recursion, and output budgets under adversarial scale.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
